name: Kagutsuchi
description: Kagutsuchi, also known as Homusubi, is a prominent fire deity in Japanese mythology. His birth is famously tragic, as his mother, Izanami, died giving birth to him, leading Izanagi to slay him in grief and rage. This act of patricide is a pivotal moment in the creation narrative of Japan, marking the separation of the divine and mortal realms, and the beginning of death. Kagutsuchi's fiery nature is both destructive and transformative, representing the raw power of fire that can consume but also purify and forge. He is associated with volcanoes, blacksmithing, and all aspects of fire. The narratives surrounding Kagutsuchi highlight the complex relationship between creation and destruction, and the inherent dangers associated with uncontrolled power. He is a testament to the potent forces at play in the formation of the world and the consequences of unchecked divine actions. Offerings were often made to Kagutsuchi to prevent fires and ensure the safety of homes and communities.
subtitle: The Incarnation of Fire

keyMyths.title: The Birth and Death of Izanami
keyMyths.description: Kagutsuchi's birth is one of the most significant events in Japanese mythology. As Izanami gave birth to him, the intense heat burned her, leading to her death and descent into Yomi, the underworld. This event sparked Izanagi's grief and anger, ultimately leading him to pursue Izanami and later sever his connection with her and the underworld. This myth highlights the destructive potential of fire and its role in separating the divine and mortal realms.

keyMyths.title: Izanagi's Rage
keyMyths.description: Consumed by grief and rage over the death of Izanami, Izanagi drew his Totsuka-no-Tsurugi (Ten-Hand Sword) and slayed Kagutsuchi. The blood that dripped from the sword created other deities associated with mountains and swords. This act of patricide is a pivotal moment, representing the consequences of unchecked emotions and the cyclical nature of creation and destruction. Even from destruction, new entities and forces emerge.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Other Deities
keyMyths.description: From the various parts of Kagutsuchi's body after being slain, new deities were born. This act of creation from destruction is a common theme in many mythologies. The deities born from Kagutsuchi's blood and remains are often associated with mountains, swords, and other natural phenomena. This further emphasizes the interconnectedness of all things and the idea that even from death, life and new beginnings can arise.
